title: pelargonium × erosum
intro: Pelargonium × erosum, also known as zonal geranium or trailing pelargonium, is a hybrid plant species within the geranium family, characterized by its trailing stems and heart-shaped leaves. The plant is used for its medicinal properties, primarily due to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to possess antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Pelargonium × erosum include reducing anxiety and stress, alleviating symptoms of the common cold, and promoting a restful sleep. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
